The v035 dispatcher-settings re-baseline reworded two of this scenario's clause lines (the `drive` 1:1 mapping sentence and the TUI drive-the-valves clause), so their gap-ids were rebound; the TUI clause now names six commands and binds to Scenario 10, whose override scene is still pending a test.

scenario: Scenario 13 -- Operator distinguishes cockpit-blind from factory-idle
reason: Top-of-pyramid render-level test asserting the idle-vs-blind header distinction: unavailable sources are counted and named in the header, and a healthy cycle shows no phantom unavailability count. Extended with four source-availability-honesty assertions (b1-source-availability-honesty): a reachable source is counted available and shows no unavailability indicator; a reached-but-empty source (an empty work-item ledger, zero open pull requests, or an absent-but-expected dispatch journal) is observed-and-idle, never counted or named unavailable; a recovered source clears from the tally on its next successful observation (latest-poll-outcome-per-source, not any historical failure); and a genuinely-unreachable source is counted and named with a durably-persisted human-readable reason. Binds the new Adapter Contract honesty clause (empty-is-not-unavailable / persisted-reason / latest-per-source).

scenario: Scenario 18 -- Operator opens pane-specific modal help and exits only with Esc
reason: Top-of-pyramid real-TUI tmux E2E (`tmux_tui_e2e_modal_help_scenario_18`) driving the shipped binary through a real tmux pane: `?` opens the overlay auto-focused to the focused pane's section (Attention, Lanes, Settings), with a Global-actions section plus one per focusable pane; the overlay is a 3-character-border near-full-viewport window (corners derived from the captured viewport) that never renders wider than the viewport; a left-side section menu beside a right-side help-text pane whose content switches as the menu is navigated and which left/right never scroll; and a modal that always prints `esc to exit` and closes only on Esc -- `?` and other keys leave it open with the underlying view unswitched and unscrolled, and Esc returns focus to the originating pane. The render/keymap/reducer slices (auto-focus section selection, Esc-only close, section navigation resetting scroll, up/down-and-PgUp/PgDn key mapping, and the 3-character border geometry) are unit-covered in console-application and console-tui; this E2E covers the shipped raw-mode render/loop path end to end.

reason: Top-of-pyramid proof of the operator status-move surface: selecting a lane work-item and confirming the move-to-status valve persists the command the shared orchestrator port dispatches. It anchors the two re-derived command-count clauses (the eight-command / seven-map-1:1 Command-Handling and TUI-Contract MUST lines), which now assert the move and resolve-blocked commands issue through the drive surface; the sibling tests move_handler_maps_each_pre_terminal_target_onto_the_move_action_id, move_handler_rejects_ship_guarded_and_malformed_targets_and_empty_ids, and valve_and_move_status_keys_fire_on_a_selected_lane_item complete the pyramid across the broad move, the ship-guard, and the picker firing on a selected lane item.

scenario: Scenario 19 -- Operator reads context-specific shortcut hints in the Status line
reason: Top-of-pyramid real-TUI tmux E2E (`tmux_tui_e2e_status_line_context_hints`) driving the shipped binary through a real tmux pane: the focused pane (Attention) renders non-empty, context-appropriate hints; switching focus (Lanes -> Settings) changes the hints AND the two panes' hint sets differ (the Lanes `move-status` key vs. the Settings `edit row` key); opening the `?` Help overlay replaces the pane's hints with the overlay's (`close help`) with the pane's `move-status` gone, and closing it (Esc) restores the pane's hints; and no context with available actions shows an empty hint line. The Status line renders in a height-3 bordered box below the Help modal's 3-row bottom margin so the overlay's hints stay visible and capturable while the modal is open. The pane/overlay hint derivation (`footer_hint` over the focused `active_view` and open `overlay`) is unit-covered in console-application; this E2E covers the shipped raw-mode render/loop path end to end. The clause now also forbids a DISHONEST hint (a key advertised where it is inert, or described by an action it does not perform in that context); that added rule is proven by the reducer-level siblings crates/console-application/src/lib.rs::the_status_hint_distinguishes_the_lane_overview_from_a_drilled_in_lane and crates/console-tui/src/lib.rs::status_hint_stops_claiming_drill_where_enter_no_longer_drills, which pin the Lanes hint to `enter drill` on the lane overview and `enter item` inside a drilled-in lane, where Enter previously did nothing while the hint still said `drill`. Independent review then found the clause's first wording was itself violated at ship time by the hints this change edits: the lane OVERVIEW advertised six per-item keys (s / p / c / r / m / n) that are inert there because its selection is a LANE and not a work-item, and an empty drilled-in lane advertised `enter item` where Enter opens nothing. The hints are now selection-aware and the clause states the enforceable rule (no per-item key where no work-item is selected) while explicitly NOT promising per-item-state suppression, which depends on the orchestrator's valid-verb vocabulary this console does not yet consume. Proven by crates/console-application/src/lib.rs::the_status_hint_distinguishes_the_lane_overview_from_a_drilled_in_lane, which now asserts the overview and the empty drilled-in lane carry no per-item key and that an empty Attention inbox drops its valve keys.

clauses.scenario: Scenario 19 -- Operator reads context-specific shortcut hints in the Status line

scenario: Scenario 20 -- Operator focuses and horizontally scrolls the top/header pane on a narrow viewport
reason: Top-of-pyramid real-TUI tmux E2E (`tmux_tui_e2e_top_pane_focus_hscroll`) driving the shipped binary through a real tmux pane: on a narrow (56-col) viewport the blurred header shrinks to fit (dropping the low-value `fleet: livespec` field, no `[focus]` marker); three Tabs cycle focus onto the top/header pane (Nav -> Content -> Detail -> Header), whose block title gains `[focus]` and whose FULL header line renders at offset 0 (fleet visible at the left, `attention:` clipped off the right); scrolling right saturates the render-measured clamp to the right edge (revealing `attention:`, panning `fleet` off-screen) and scrolling left returns to the left edge; blur (Tab off the header) snaps the pane back to its shrink-to-fit, left-justified default; and a wide launch shows the whole header with a zero scroll clamp. The focus-ring cycle, header-scroll clamp/reset, focused-vs-blurred render, `footer()` header hints, and the `?`-to-header-section behavior are unit-covered in console-application and console-tui; this E2E covers the shipped raw-mode render/loop path end to end.

clauses.scenario: Scenario 20 -- Operator focuses and horizontally scrolls the top/header pane on a narrow viewport

scenario: Scenario 21 -- Operator sees panes render operational content only, no baked-in documentation prose
reason: Top-of-pyramid real-TUI tmux E2E (`tmux_tui_e2e_panes_operational_content_only_scenario_21`) driving the shipped binary through a real tmux pane: each swept view (Attention, Spec, Lanes, Events, Repos, Settings) renders its operational content only -- the Spec pane shows its live counts (`LiveSpec next snapshots:`, `Revise required:`), the Events pane its stored-event count (`Stored events:`), the Repos pane its roster (`Repos observed:`) -- while a sweep of every pane body finds none of the removed documentation sentences ('Spec lifecycle status is projected from LiveSpec adapter observations.', 'Revise-required events stay visible in the Spec view until resolved.', 'The event log is the canonical source for projections.'), asserted via short distinctive fragments ('is projected from', 'stay visible', 'canonical source') that would render on a single line if present so detail-pane wrapping cannot hide a regression, and that render in no KEPT operational-help surface (the Status-line hints, the modal Help overlay, the Settings per-row inline help). Tier: top-of-pyramid acceptance, under crates/console-cli/tests/. The §"TUI Contract" panes-operational-content-only clause binds here.

clauses.scenario: Scenario 21 -- Operator sees panes render operational content only, no baked-in documentation prose

scenario: Scenario 22 -- User-facing documentation lives in the docs/ tree with the README as a pointer
reason: Top-of-pyramid structural coverage for the User Documentation Contract, in crates/console-completeness-check/tests/scenario_22_user_docs_tree.rs: the top-level README.md links docs/README.md and carries no user-facing documentation sections of its own while retaining contributor material (readme_is_a_pointer_carrying_no_user_facing_documentation, readme_retains_contributor_facing_material); docs/README.md is an overview plus a table of contents linking each sub-document by relative path (docs_index_is_an_overview_and_table_of_contents); the tree carries the four named sub-documents, with installing covering the download-install path and running the console against a repository other than its own, and detailed-usage carrying a section per TUI pane (docs_tree_carries_the_four_required_sub_documents, installing_covers_download_install_and_another_repository, detailed_usage_carries_a_section_per_tui_pane); and the settings doc the completeness check reads is docs/detailed-usage.md rather than the README, which carries the Dispatcher-settings section the check scopes to (settings_doc_is_the_detailed_usage_sub_document, settings_doc_carries_the_dispatcher_settings_section). The test lives in the completeness-check crate because the settings-doc case binds the contract to the SETTINGS_DOC constant that crate actually reads -- the surface that would silently drift if the contract and the gate disagreed.

scenario: Scenario 23 -- Operator reads a work-item's full record without leaving the console
reason: Top-of-pyramid render proof of the work-item record drill-in, in crates/console-tui/src/lib.rs: a fixture work-item with EVERY standardized field populated is projected through the real lane board and rendered, and each field (title, description, type, status, lane, rank, origin, gap_id, assignee, depends_on, captured_at, resolution, reason, audit, superseded_by, spec_commitment_hint) plus its label is asserted present (work_item_detail_modal_renders_every_standardized_record_field). The sibling tests complete the scenario: work_item_detail_modal_shows_absent_fields_as_absent_and_handles_no_selection (an unemitted field renders as the explicit absent placeholder, the item still lists in its lane, and a no-selection / too-small viewport degrades without panicking); work_item_detail_modal_scrolls_a_long_description_to_its_bottom (a description taller than the viewport reaches its last row and clamps there, with the close hint pinned on its reserved row at every offset); enter_opens_the_item_modal_and_esc_closes_it_back_to_the_lane (Enter on a drilled-in lane row opens the record and Esc unwinds exactly one level, back to the lane rather than the lane overview); item_modal_scroll_keys_move_the_offset_and_are_inert_elsewhere (up/down and PgUp/PgDn scroll it, Enter and `?` are inert over the read-only surface); status_hint_stops_claiming_drill_where_enter_no_longer_drills (the Status line names the item drill-in inside a drilled-in lane instead of the lane drill-in it used to claim while Enter was inert); and the_open_item_modal_draws_over_the_whole_screen (the record draws through the real render path over the lane board). The plumbing that feeds the surface is proven in crates/console-application/src/source_adapters.rs by parse_orchestrator_carries_the_whole_standardized_record (every descriptive field survives the adapter, with the structured depends_on and audit shapes flattened and an unrecognized dependency element degraded rather than dropped), work_item_detail_survives_the_payload_round_trip (the persisted payload and its read-back move in lockstep, and a pre-record ledger row still replays), an_edited_description_appends_a_fresh_observation (a description edit reaches the console instead of sitting stale behind an unchanged lifecycle state, while an unchanged record stays idempotent), and a_record_missing_every_descriptive_field_still_observes (a leaner emission keeps the work-item on the board rather than tripping the fail-soft skip).

clauses.scenario: Scenario 23 -- Operator reads a work-item's full record without leaving the console
